members.name: writeCount
members.description: The number of times data has been written. This can be used to create an incrementing filename.

methods.name: sendBytes
methods.description: Write one or more sequence of bytes. No terminators are appended. *message - Messages can any combination of strings, byte arrays, or individual single-byte numeric values. To serialize non-byte values (example floats or integers) there are several python modules such as pickle or struct.

methods.name: send
methods.description: Used to write strings into the file. *messages - One or more strings to write into the file. *terminator - (Keyword, Optional) Specifies how the message is to be terminated. If no append terminator is specified, a null character will automatically be appended to the message. To send no terminator, use terminator=. The number of bytes sent is returned.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the FileoutDAT Class wiki page
